passage: Not only the district superintendent but also the members of the school board has acknowledged that the reading scores reported in the annual performance review are significantly below state benchmarks. A comprehensive literacy initiative is being developed in response.
question: Which choice best corrects the subject-verb agreement error in the first sentence?

choices.A: Not only the district superintendent but also the members of the school board have acknowledged that the reading scores reported in the annual performance review are significantly below state benchmarks.
choices.B: Not only the district superintendent but also the members of the school board has acknowledged that reading scores are significantly below state benchmarks.
choices.C: Not only the district superintendent but also the member of the school board has acknowledged that reading scores are below state benchmarks.
choices.D: No change; 'has acknowledged' correctly agrees with 'the district superintendent,' the subject in a not only/but also construction

explanation: In 'not only...but also' constructions, the verb agrees with the subject closest to it (the nearer subject). The nearer subject is 'the members of the school board,' where 'members' is a plural noun. The plural verb 'have acknowledged' is required. Choice A uses 'have acknowledged.' Choice B preserves 'has acknowledged.' Choice C changes 'members' to 'member' (singular) and uses 'has acknowledged'—internally consistent but changes the meaning. Choice D incorrectly applies the nearer-subject rule to 'the district superintendent,' which is the farther subject.
